Composed-block traced tracy (metal-trace captured+replayed, fp32 [1,1,896,1536] replicated 1x4 mesh, production operating point) exposed a layout-interaction stall invisible in sub-block isolation: 5.5MB fp32 L1-interleaved norm output stalls every consuming ttnn.linear (QKV 2963us, fc1 2650us, fc3 2637us vs 97-134us for DRAM-fed o_proj/fc2) - the skill's 'never pin a LARGE activation to L1 before a matmul' counter-example; rmsnorm's earlier isolated -25% win reverses ~25x in composition. Per-device block kernel time 9632.5 -> 1943.7us (-79.8%); QKV 199, fc1 172, fc3 174, norms 66->90us (+24 expected DRAM write). PCC re-verified: vision_block 0.999958, vision_rmsnorm 0.999982 (both unchanged). Guard ok. Dispatched inline (no Agent tool in tick context).

Top hotspot post-change MatmulDeviceOperation (up-proj 6144x6144) 425.9us 52.2% of block kernel time at 96c, fp32 HiFi4 compute-bound - no per-block lever left without precision risk. KB entries for kind mlp reviewed: ttnn_gelu already applied in ttnn phase (exact erf standalone; fuse-into-matmul variant rejected for PCC per entry note); ttnn_silu_2/ttnn_mul_1 (SwiGLU) and ttnn_concat/ttnn_fold n/a to LayerNorm->Linear->GELU->Linear - none newly applied. All real fp32 vision_tower weights, replicated on 1x4 mesh per parallelism plan (placement=replicate); seq padded 784->896, host rope/cu_seqlens per hybrid_notes. Found+worked around: ttnn.rms_norm fp32 ROW_MAJOR gamma is misread on device (PCC ~0) -> fp32 gammas use TILE [1,1,1,dim]. PCC 0.990121 — thin margin over 0.99; per-block isolation PCC 0.99999+, deficit is amplified early-layer SDPA-core bf16 noise (bf16-only kernel). bf16 paths of the four modified sub-block files re-verified: patch_embed 0.999957, rmsnorm 0.999982, attention 0.999855, block 0.999958 — all unchanged. Guard ok (lint 0, kernels ok, no new host ops). KB entries for kind other were generic eltwise/scatter records — none applicable, none used. Dispatched inline (no Agent tool in tick context); worker contract followed verbatim.

optimization.notes: REDO done, both steps. Step1 dtype: tower fp32->bf16 acts+weights (HiFi4+fp32acc matmuls); 11k-token shape (/tmp/demo_image1_cropped.jpg seq 11224) 3073ms, tracy traced==untraced (compute-bound), WindowedSDPA 86.9% (62.6ms/call, kernel default 32x32 chunks). Fix: SDPA HiFi2+fp32acc q256k256 on 11x10 grid (bf16 dest acc rejected: bf16 softmax*V accumulation over 11k tokens) -> 889ms untraced, kernel 779ms, target<=900 met; fp32 high-precision path keeps kernel defaults (0.9901 margin untouched). Step2 TP A/B: head-parallel TP4 (QKV col-shard chip-blocked 3 heads/chip, o_proj row-shard, MLP col/row, RS+AG all-reduce; CCL 1.3ms/op) 485.9ms vs replicate 889ms -> TP4 kept in ocr_model (degenerates tp=1 single device). E2E parity gate (5-sample WER vs HF) PASSED after EACH step: 0.0000==HF, 5/5 exact; block PCC informational 0.9821 bf16 / 0.9852 TP4 (gate redefined to E2E per redo). Sub-block bf16 PCC unchanged (attn 0.999824 mlp 0.999999 block 0.999955); lint 0 all touched. Final tracy 470.6ms kernel: AllGather 23.4%, SDPA 23.0%, RS 21.6%, Matmul 19.7% — next lever async CCL. Dispatched inline (no Agent tool in tick context); worker contract followed.

optimization.notes: Tracy-driven single change: replicated-path ttnn.rms_norm switched from default DRAM-interleaved (4-core row-tile-capped LayerNorm kernel at seq 128) to WIDTH-SHARDED 4x3 grid (12 cores, block_w=4, LayerNormShardedMultiCoreProgramConfig) with an i2s/s2i bounce that keeps the DRAM-interleaved block-output contract (both decoder_layer consumers are matmuls; tick-23 L1-interleaved-into-matmul stall counter-example forbids an L1 pin). Shape-gated (tile-aligned seq, dim tiles %12==0, per-core shard <=256KB) with deterministic shape-keyed config cache; non-qualifying shapes (decode seq=1) fall back to the fused interleaved path. Traced tracy at the production operating point (fp32 [1,1,128,1536] replicated 1x4 mesh, metal-trace captured+replayed, 2 replay sessions): per-device block kernel time 21.1us -> 16.5us (-22%); LayerNormDeviceOperation 21.1us@4c -> 8.2us@12c, +i2s 3.8us +s2i 4.5us@12c. Wall-clock A/B over 200 traced replays, 2 reps each: 24.4/24.3 -> 21.1/21.1 us/iter (-13.4%); grids 8x6 (24.4), 8x3 (22.6), 6x2 (21.7), 2x3 (26.0) all measured worse than 4x3. Top hotspot post-change LayerNormDeviceOperation ~50% of block kernel time at 12c. Halving weight bytes moved it to 245-253us/device (-29%); HiFi4+fp32-acc accumulation unchanged. use_cases.name: ocr
use_cases.description: Document OCR / layout parsing: image plus text prompt in, structured text (markdown/JSON) out via autoregressive decode.
use_cases.hybrid_notes: Image preprocessing (resize, patchify, grid_thw) and tokenizer/chat-template stay on HF host; rot_pos_emb tables and cu_seqlens computed on host.

generation.notes: REDO complete (decode_strategy=kv_cache): full-sequence-recompute AR loop replaced by a KV-cached token step. text_attention gained init_kv_cache (persistent per-chip fp32 K/V [1,hpd,max_seq,hd]; kv_replication=2 - each Q-head group holds a full copy of its KV head's cache, chip-local attention, no CCL in attention) plus a decode path beside prefill: QKV linear -> nlp_create_qkv_heads -> explicit fp32 rope -> ttnn.experimental.paged_update_cache (single contiguous cache, persistent int32 slot buffer shared across all 28 layers, seamless_m4t recipe) -> single-row fp32 attention over the cache with a streamed [1,1,1,max_seq] mask. Prefill runs ONCE (ttnn.fill_cache of post-rope K/V slots 0..P32-1; first token from prompt-row logits); decoder_layer.forward_decode wires norms/MLP around the token row; ocr_model holds persistent caches (max_cache_seq 3200) and prefill+step loop. The bf16-only scaled_dot_product_attention_decode kernel was rejected for the established Qwen2 attention-sink reason (+-3122 logits, bf16 SDPA PCC ~0.92 prefill); fp32 explicit core is the model's SDPA-decode idiom; paged_update_cache supports fp32 caches (cache-row check vs torch: PCC ~1.0). Parity gate PASS: WER 0.0000 (TTNN) vs HF 0.0000, tolerance 0.05, all 5 samples exact. 5-sample set + long sample prefilled: steady decode 89.8-90.5 ms/tok at gate shapes and 90.2 ms/tok at 2305-token context (prefill 266.8 ms once) - per-token cost flat in context (was ~90 ms/tok x O(L) recompute). Step is host-dispatch-bound; perf phase traces the token step (target <=20 ms/tok at 3k). Guard verify_use_case ok. Dispatched inline (no Agent tool in tick context); worker contract followed verbatim.

perf.notes: REDO 2 (decode weight dtype) complete: QKV/o_proj fp32->bf16 and MLP gate/up bf16->bf8 (down bf16, lm_head fp32, fp32 attention activations/accum + fp32 KV cache unchanged), each step re-validated against the e2e gate (WER 0.0000 vs HF 0.0000, 5/5 exact). Wall-clock A/B over 200 traced replays: 18.03 -> 18.07 ms/tok (flat, within noise); traced tracy kernel sum 17.14 -> 16.14 ms/replay per-GCC; matmul slice flat at 8.6 ms over 197 calls. DRAM-bound-on-fp32-weights hypothesis REJECTED with traced evidence: 1-row decode matmuls are dispatch/latency bound; dtype lever exhausted (kept: parity-neutral, frees ~250 MB/chip). <=12 ms/tok needs host-floor (~6 ms) + fused-op work (fused rotary, fused silu-mul, all-reduce); see PERF_NOTES.md tick-50 section. Guard parity re-run PASS.
